description.value:
Only a single copy known to exist—a set of fired clay tablets inscribed with Babylonian cuneiform. Legend claims the text, a copy and translation of an older Sumerian text, was found engraved on a large and hideous idol taken as plunder by Sargon of Akkad during the Elamite Conquest. Identified as prayers and supplications to supernatural allies, Sargon ordered it translated and spells cast. Afterward, he ordered the idol smashed and the translating magi executed. How this copy survived is unknown.

LanguageBabylonian cuneiform
AuthorUnknown (possibly translated from older Sumerian text)
DateAncient origin
Sanity Loss1D6
Cthulhu Mythos+1/+2 percentiles
Mythos Rating9
Study (weeks)8

Spells Contained

  • Entreat the Eaters of Carrion (Contact Ghoul)
  • Entreat Those Who Hide Within the Storm (Contact Sand-Dweller)
